key_themes: Strong AUM growth to £30.6bn with significant net inflows especially in tech & AI funds
£15m share buyback program with multiple recent tranches and share cancellations
Robust fund performance and earnings momentum driving new 52-week highs
Attractive valuation, high ~6.6% dividend yield, and analyst price targets (750-773p)
Boutique asset manager appeal with long-term investor accumulation
summary: Polar Capital Holdings (POLR.L), a UK specialist asset manager, reported strong growth with AUM rising to £30.6bn (+43% YoY) driven by £1.44bn Q4 net inflows and positive performance in tech/AI strategies. The company launched a £15m share buyback, continues cancelling repurchased shares, and the stock has rallied sharply (YTD +33%, 1Y +93%) to new highs around 708p with a 6.6% dividend yield. Investor and analyst sentiment is strongly bullish, citing undervaluation on a ~12x PER, earnings momentum, and the firm's boutique model.
sources: 15+ recent X posts (mostly bullish investor commentary and research shares), 12+ news/RNS articles (Yahoo Finance, LSE announcements, Morningstar, company updates from Jan-Apr 2026)
